title: ELDER SAFE PROGRAM - 211info
text: WASHINGTON COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE ELDER SAFE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps older adults after they report a crime and continues to help them through the criminal justice process.Volunteer victim advocates provide crime victims with emotional support and information relating to the criminal justice system and their case. Assistance may include personal support, court advocacy, or help filling out forms. 215 SW Adams Avenue Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: Age 65 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 846-2773 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 846-2773 Elder Safe Assistant Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None AREA SERVED: Washington County SITE HOURS: Law enforcement services: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Max Blue Line Services: Elder Abuse Prevention General Crime Victim Assistance * Older Adults Other Services Offered At This Location: CONSUMER ASSISTANCE AND PROTECTION - LAW ENFORCEMENT COMPLAINTS CRIMINAL CORRECTIONAL SYSTEM - JAIL INFORMATION LAW ENFORCEMENT AGENCIES - SHERIFF'S OFFICE LAW ENFORCEMENT SERVICES - COMMUNITY RESOURCES AND EDUCATION LAW ENFORCEMENT SERVICES - HELP ME HOME If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. WASHINGTON COUNTY SHERIFF'S OFFICE
